Politics.headline: Oregon Voters Overwhelmingly Said Yes to Limiting Money in Politics. Then Politicians Had Their Say.
Politics.detail: In 2020, 78% of Oregon voters approved limiting campaign contributions; however, recent legislative changes have rendered these limits 'illusory' according to an advocate for tighter controls. The Legislature finally adopted limits four years later, but an advocate for tighter controls says recent changes render Oregon's contribution limits “illusory.”

Politics.headline: Bleak economic data shows UK plc in trouble well before Middle East crisis.
Politics.detail: Recent economic data indicates that the UK economy was struggling even before the current crisis in the Middle East. The article is published 4 hours ago.

Politics.headline: 'I don't give a shit about Iran. I don't want to pay higher gas': Motor City motorists feel pinch as gas prices surge.
Politics.detail: Drivers in Detroit express unhappiness with rising gas prices, with mixed reactions to the US-Israel war on Iran. This article was published recently.

Politics.headline: The Pentagon is turning procurement into policy, staking its claim as Washington's most powerful AI player with its decision to ditch Anthropic.
Politics.detail: The Pentagon is using its procurement power to shape AI policy, demonstrated by its decision to move away from Anthropic. The Pentagon is turning procurement into policy, staking its claim as Washington's most powerful AI player with its decision to ditch Anthropic.

Politics.headline: In linking Iran to Russia, Healey could be laying ground for hard choices ahead.
Politics.detail: Defence Secretary John Healey's statements linking Iran to Russia may be preparing the ground for difficult decisions in the future. The article is published 4 hours ago.

Finance & Economy.headline: US stocks erase a big early loss and rise after oil prices whip from nearly $120 back below $90
Finance & Economy.detail: After a sharp decline, U.S. stocks rebounded as oil prices fluctuated dramatically due to the ongoing Iran war, initially surging to $120 before falling back below $90.

Finance & Economy.headline: Crude oil prices surpass $100 a barrel as the Iran war impedes production and shipping
Finance & Economy.detail: The Iran war has disrupted crude oil production and shipping, causing prices to rise above $100 a barrel.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's 'roaring' economy meets a rough start to 2026: What the latest numbers show
Finance & Economy.detail: Recent economic data indicates a challenging start to 2026, despite claims of a strong economy under the Trump administration.

Finance & Economy.headline: World shares decline, while oil pops above $100 a barrel over Iran war worries
Finance & Economy.detail: Global stock markets are declining, and Brent crude oil prices have risen above $100 per barrel due to concerns about the impact of the Iran war on crude oil and gas supplies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Market swings? Advisors say stick to your plan, not your emotions
Finance & Economy.detail: Financial advisors recommend maintaining a long-term investment strategy and avoiding impulsive decisions during volatile market conditions influenced by events such as the Iran war.

Technology.headline: IATA Announces World Data Symposium 2026 in Singapore to Accelerate Aviation's Data, Technology & Cybersecurity Transformation
Technology.detail: The International Air Transport Association (IATA) will hold its 2026 edition of the World Data Symposium (WDS) in Singapore on 8–9 April, bringing together data, technology, and cybersecurity leaders.

Technology.headline: China's new 5-year plan bets on tech innovation built on energy transition, security
Technology.detail: China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030) emphasizes technology innovation and digitalization to rebuild growth, with a focus on energy transition and security.

Technology.headline: PhocusWire's travel tech news briefs: Airbnb, RateGain, Lighthouse and more...
Technology.detail: This week's travel tech news includes Key Data launching Dex AI, an AI tool for short-term rental property managers, and Dolphin Dynamics launching an AI helper to cut down on manual data entry for travel companies.

Technology.headline: Weekly Intelligence Report – 13 March 2026
Technology.detail: CYFIRMA's research team discovered Chip Ransomware, a file-encrypting malware associated with the Medusa Locker ransomware family, which encrypts victim files and appends the ".chip1" extension. The report also highlights the Amos Stealer campaign targeting macOS devices.

Technology.headline: Tech News Roundup - 13.03.26
Technology.detail: Digital agency HeadFirst celebrates double award win, recognizing their strategy, design, and technology expertise in building digital platforms across various sectors. Co-CEO Nathan Baranowski notes the accelerating impact of AI and emerging technologies on how organizations operate.

Science.headline: Study Finds Two Types of Colon Polyps Can Raise Bowel Cancer Risk Fivefold
Science.detail: Researchers studying over 8,400 colonoscopies discovered that having both adenomas and serrated polyps in the bowel can raise the risk of serious precancerous changes by up to five times, suggesting these polyp types may represent separate cancer pathways.

Science.headline: Scientists at Arizona State University have uncovered surprising new ways bacteria move, even without their usual whip-like propellers called flagella
Science.detail: In one study, E. coli and salmonella were found to spread across moist surfaces by fermenting.

Science.headline: A single dose of psilocybin is more effective than nicotine patches for quitting smoking, study suggests
Science.detail: A recent study indicates that a single dose of psilocybin may be more effective than nicotine patches for individuals looking to quit smoking.

Science.headline: Global observations reveal rapid reorganization of ocean nutrients
Science.detail: New global observations show a rapid reorganization of ocean nutrients.

Science.headline: Fantastic fungi found with ability to freeze water
Science.detail: Researchers have discovered fungi with the ability to freeze water.

Health.headline: Monty Python Got It Wrong About Medieval Disease
Health.detail: In medieval Denmark, people could pay for more prestigious graves closer to the church, indicating wealth and status, which provides insights into disease and social hierarchy.

Health.headline: New "Super Antibiotic" Stops Deadly Gut Infection Without Destroying the Microbiome
Health.detail: A new antibiotic has been developed that can stop a deadly gut infection without harming the good bacteria in the microbiome.

Health.headline: Trump policies set to increase rates of lung disease and death, study finds.
Health.detail: A study has found that policies enacted during the Trump administration are expected to increase the rates of lung disease and death.

Health.headline: Researchers studying over 8,400 colonoscopies discovered that having both adenomas and serrated polyps in the bowel can raise the risk of serious precancerous changes by up to five times.
Health.detail: A study analyzing over 8,400 colonoscopies found that individuals with both adenomas and serrated polyps in the bowel face a significantly elevated risk (up to five times) of developing serious precancerous changes.

Health.headline: A severe case of COVID-19 or influenza could increase the risk of lung cancer later on, according to new research.
Health.detail: New research indicates that experiencing a severe case of COVID-19 or influenza may elevate the risk of developing lung cancer later in life due to alterations in immune cells caused by the viral infections.

Sports.headline: Russell takes pole for China GP sprint race in Mercedes front-row lockout
Sports.detail: George Russell dominated qualifying for the Chinese Grand Prix sprint race, leading a Mercedes front-row lockout. He comfortably beat his teammate Kimi Antonelli to pole position.

Sports.headline: Bans will cost Mourinho two games as Benfica calls punishment 'unfair'
Sports.detail: José Mourinho will be suspended for two games, a decision Benfica is calling unfair. Additional details regarding the reason for the ban were not specified in the search results.

Sports.headline: Elliott and Delson win Paralympic banked slalom gold
Sports.detail: Team USA's Noah Elliott and Delson earned gold medals in the Paralympic banked slalom event. The event took place at the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Paralympics.

Sports.headline: Chychrun's late winner lifts Capitals, snaps Sabres' 8-game win streak
Sports.detail: Chychrun scored a late goal to help the Washington Capitals defeat the Sabres, ending their eight-game winning streak. Additional details about the game were not specified in the search results.

Sports.headline: Vikings sign Kyler Murray in their latest QB pivot, bringing in competition for J.J. McCarthy
Sports.detail: The Minnesota Vikings have signed Kyler Murray to a one-year contract, worth $36.8 million, creating competition at the quarterback position with J.J. McCarthy. Murray will seek to get a head start on the 2027 free agency cycle.

Entertainment.headline: Jack Osbourne welcomes baby daughter named in touching tribute to Ozzy Osbourne
Entertainment.detail: Jack Osbourne has named his newborn daughter Ozzy, in a touching tribute to his father, the legendary rocker Ozzy Osbourne.

Entertainment.headline: Nicole Kidman breaks silence on life after Keith Urban divorce
Entertainment.detail: Nicole Kidman has finally spoken out about her life after her split from Keith Urban.

Entertainment.headline: Snoop Dogg, pigsty fights and the wrong kind of snow: Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan on making the Peaky Blinders movie
Entertainment.detail: Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan discuss the making of the Peaky Blinders movie, including details about Snoop Dogg, pigsty fights, and other interesting aspects of the film.

Entertainment.headline: 'Wouldn't life be easier if I were white?': inside a provocative race-swap body horror
Entertainment.detail: An exploration of a new body horror film that deals with themes of racial identity and the question of whether life would be easier if the protagonist were white.

Entertainment.headline: 'Border 2' OTT release: Here's when and where you can watch
Entertainment.detail: Sunny Deol's 'Border 2', which earned Rs 392cr+ worldwide after its January 23, 2026 release, will be available on OTT platforms from March 20, directed by Anurag Singh, the film features Varun Dhawan, Diljit Dosanjh, and Ahan Shetty.

Education.headline: AI toys for young children must be more tightly regulated, say researchers
Education.detail: Researchers are calling for stricter regulations on AI-powered toys for young children, citing concerns about data privacy and potential developmental impacts.

Education.headline: ROTC students at Old Dominion University subdued and killed shooter who left 1 dead, 2 hurt
Education.detail: ROTC students at Old Dominion University subdued and killed a shooter, identified as Mohamed Bailor Jalloh, who pleaded guilty in 2016 to attempting to provide material support to the Islamic State, after he shot and killed one person and hurt two others.

Education.headline: Panic buttons in schools: $7 million effort is delayed a year
Education.detail: A $7 million effort to install panic buttons in schools has been delayed by a year.

Education.headline: English universities 'face £5.6 billion bill to fix broken labs'
Education.detail: English universities are facing a significant financial challenge, needing an estimated £5.6 billion to repair and update deteriorating laboratory facilities.

Education.headline: Short tempers and legal threats: UK teachers report rise in problem parents
Education.detail: UK teachers are reporting an increase in instances of short tempers and legal threats from parents, creating a challenging environment for educators.

Lifestyle.headline: Singaporean actress Kym Ng loves running on weekends as it makes her feel free like a child
Lifestyle.detail: Singaporean actress Kym Ng enjoys running on weekends because it gives her a sense of freedom.

Lifestyle.headline: Sharon Cuneta undergoes spine procedure in Singapore
Lifestyle.detail: Veteran singer-actress Sharon Cuneta revealed that she recently underwent a spine procedure in Singapore after suffering from persistent pain affecting her hip and leg for nearly two years and is currently recovering.

Lifestyle.headline: 2 Filipino restaurants land spots on Asia's 50 Best Restaurants 2026 extended list
Lifestyle.detail: Two Michelin-starred restaurants in Metro Manila, Celera and Toyo Eatery, have been included in the Asia's 50 Best Restaurants 2026 extended list, ranking at 100th and 71st respectively.

Lifestyle.headline: Grand Launch of Desire Designer Exhibition
Lifestyle.detail: The Desire Designer Exhibition, showcasing fashion, jewelry, and lifestyle products from designers across India, is being held in Hyderabad from March 13-15, 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: Five ways to better manage your time (and avoid stress)
Lifestyle.detail: Tips on managing time effectively to reduce stress and improve overall well-being.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The king's visit to the US must go ahead despite Trump's terrible military aggression
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Simon Jenkins argues that King Charles's visit to the US should proceed despite the ongoing military aggression by President Trump.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Spies in plain sight
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the Alice Guo case, highlighting it as a template used to bestow Filipino citizenship on illegal Chinese nationals involved in criminal activities.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The 'excursionists' in the US-Israel war with Iran
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A column discusses about US-Israel war with Iran.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Kathleen Parker: The GOP is getting a makeover — just in time for the midterms.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Kathleen Parker discusses about the GOP getting a makeover for the midterms.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Contributor: What a U.S. victory would look like in the Iran war
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discusses about what a U.S. victory would look like in the Iran war.

World.headline: 4 U.S. Service Members Killed as Refueling Plane Crashes In Iraq
World.detail: A U.S. Air Force refueler crashed in Iraq while supporting the Iran war, resulting in the death of four service members; the cause was reported as not due to hostile fire. The KC-135 tanker was involved in an apparent accident with another KC-135.

World.headline: Israel launches new attacks on Tehran; 2 killed by drone debris in Oman
World.detail: Israel has launched new attacks on Tehran, and drone debris has killed two people in Oman. Tensions are rising as Iran retaliates across the Gulf, disrupting Hormuz and pushing oil prices higher.

World.headline: Michigan synagogue and Virginia university shootings: What we know
World.detail: An assailant rammed his vehicle into Detroit's Temple Israel synagogue and has been killed. Details are emerging about a shooting at a Virginia university as well.

World.headline: Turkiye says NATO defences intercepted third missile from Iran
World.detail: Turkey reports that NATO defenses intercepted a third missile launched from Iran. Video footage shows a projectile near a Turkish base hosting US troops.

World.headline: Canada lost 84K jobs as unemployment rate rose to 6.7% in February: StatCan
World.detail: Canada's economy experienced a loss of 84,000 jobs, causing the unemployment rate to increase to 6.7% in February, according to Statistics Canada. The job losses were lower than expected, with over 100,000 full-time positions lost and a decrease of 73,000 in private-sector employment.

Zimbabwe.headline: Econet to pay US$24m to exiting shareholders
Zimbabwe.detail: ECONET Wireless Zimbabwe will pay more than US$24 million in cash to investors who opted to exit.

Zimbabwe.headline: Single-digit inflation target still possible: Minister
Zimbabwe.detail: The Minister believes that a single-digit inflation target is still achievable.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZTA blitz shuts down 80 non-compliant tourism facilities
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Tourism Authority (ZTA) has shut down 80 tourism facilities for failing to comply with regulations.

Zimbabwe.headline: RBZ gets strong response to ZiG awareness campaigns
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) is reporting a strong response to its campaigns to raise awareness of the Zimbabwe Gold (ZiG) currency.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mauritius-based firm gets CTC nod to acquire Dendairy
Zimbabwe.detail: A Mauritius-based firm, ETG Group, has received approval from the Competition and Tariff Commission (CTC) to acquire Dendairy.
